design_philosophy.core_loop: Investigate curse-afflicted estate, solve supernatural mysteries through exploration and combat, defeat corrupted family members and their curse's manifestation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Unraveling the betrayal at the heart of a cursed family while battling their monstrous reanimated forms across time and dimensional rifts
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Paranormal investigator solving dark occult mysteries as a 1920s adventurer, wielding supernatural weapons against eldritch horrors
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Authored by Clive Barker; commits fully to horror-driven narrative within FPS framework; traversal of time and alternate dimensions integrated into plot progression rather than as mechanical gimmick; emphasizes atmospheric exploration and story revelation over pure combat

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition (notably the Scythe of the Celts as mandatory progression tool); supernatural spells/abilities implied
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression; retrieval of Gel'ziabar Stone and Scythe unlocks new areas and enables boss defeats; temporal/dimensional travel unlocked through plot advancement
replayability_hooks: Multiple boss encounters with distinct supernatural antagonists
Environmental puzzle solving tied to curse mythology
Dimensional/temporal traversal creates distinct level themes

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling (torture chambers, monastery corruption, standing stones ritual site)
Voice acting (Clive Barker as Ambrose Covenant)
Time travel narrative integration revealing historical context of curse

boss_design.philosophy: Encounter with corrupted family members serves dual purpose: combat challenge and narrative revelation; each boss represents a specific form of curse manifestation; final confrontation with Undying King represents culmination of temporal/dimensional mystery
notable_bosses: Lizbeth Covenant (undead form, defeated in family catacombs)
Ambrose Covenant (transforms into hulking ogre-like monster after obtaining Gel'ziabar Stone)
Count Otto Keisinger (rival sorcerer, enslaver of Oneiros dimension)
Aaron Covenant (intangible ghost requiring body location before defeat)
Bethany Covenant (traps player in primeval dimension Eternal Autumn)
Jeremiah Covenant (betrayal twist; final sealed-away form)
Undying King (final boss; insectoid monstrosity sealed away by Celtic druids, player must prevent/defeat dimensional breach)

faction_indicators: Covenant family corruption (individual members represent different ideological expressions of curse)
Monastic order corrupted by Scythe presence (benevolent faith twisted to murderous fanaticism)
Keisinger's enslaved Oneiros inhabitants (represents tyrannical control philosophy)
danger_signals: Ritual at standing stones as original sin
Madness and death spreading through family members
environmental_storytelling: Ancient standing stones as curse locus
Covenant family library containing occult grimoire
Private torture chamber revealing sibling betrayal
Celtic burial site as dimensional seal
